text: A whetstone run silently produces wrong verdicts when the frozen acceptance checks are not reachable from the worktree's start ref. Observed 2026-07-15 (run conductor-20260714-175828): setup committed the frozen checks to the LOCAL baseline branch but did not push; the generated setup:worktree worker resolves its start ref from the REMOTE, so the worktree lacked evals/<id>.test.mjs. Consequences: one fixer's good change scored 'unresolved' (checkpoint ran the in-worktree check command -> MODULE_NOT_FOUND) and another fixer copied the check into the worktree to make it runnable, tripping the evals/** surface lock -> good change 'reverted'. Fix in two layers: (1) SKILL.md setup step 6/8 must push the freeze commit (or verify the launch ref contains it) before launching; (2) scaffold-improve.cjs's Baseline phase must assert every items[].acceptanceCheck target file EXISTS in the worktree and throw with a clear message if not — failing fast beats two corrupted verdicts. Discriminating check (RED now, GREEN after): generate a loop and assert the emitted Baseline worker prompt (or a setup assertion) verifies the acceptanceCheck files' existence in the worktree; today it only runs the floor.
